info.title: Cisco Crosswork Network Controller — L3VPN
info.description:
Cisco Crosswork Network Controller (CNC) L3VPN service implements the IETF L3VPN NTW model for provisioning layer 3 VPN services across network elements.

Authentication: Bearer JWT — obtain via a two-step CAS exchange: (1) POST /crosswork/sso/v1/tickets with credentials to get a TGT, (2) POST the TGT URL with service=https://<cnc-host>/app-dashboard to receive the JWT. Paste the JWT as the bearer token. JWTs expire and must be refreshed manually until IPSO-9866 is resolved.

Source: https://developer.cisco.com/docs/crosswork/

parameters.name: vpn-service-vpn-id
parameters.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.
This identifier has a local meaning, e.g., within
a service provider network.

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description: limit the depth of nodes in response

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: optionally identify specific data nodes in response

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: xpath expression to filter data nodes in response

parameters.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.
This identifier has a local meaning, e.g., within
a service provider network.

parameters.name: insert
parameters.description: controlling the order when adding new list elements

parameters.name: point
parameters.description: used to specify the insertion point

parameters.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.

parameters.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.
This identifier has a local meaning, e.g., within

vpn-node.description: Includes a list of VPN nodes. (list)

vpn-node-id.description: An identifier of the VPN node. (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:custom-template.description: Custom templates to apply when device is ready (list)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:variable.description: Variables to bind when applying template one time (list)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:iteration.description: Apply custom template multiple times with iteration (list)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:variable.description: Variables to bind when applying template in iteration (list)

vpn-instance-profile.description:
Includes a list of active VPN instance
profiles. (list)

rd.description: Indicates an RD value that is explicitly assigned. (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:locator-name.description: SRv6 locator name, should match locators configured at a node-global level on each router (leaf)

address-family.description: Set of parameters per address family. (list)

vpn-policies.description:
VPN service policies.  'vpn-policies' contains references
to the import and export policies to be associated with
the VPN service. (non-presence)

vpn-target.description:
RTs.  AND/OR operations may be defined based on the
assigned RTs. (list)

vpn-targets.description:
Set of route targets to match for import and export
routes to/from VRF. (non-presence)

address-family.description: Indicates the address family (IPv4 and/or IPv6). (leaf)

local-as.description:
Provider's Autonomous System (AS) number.  Used if the
customer requests BGP routing. (leaf)

active-vpn-instance-profiles.description: Container for active VPN instance profiles. (non-presence)

routing-protocol.description:
List of routing protocols used on the
CE-PE link.  This list can be augmented. (list)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:mpls-deactivation.description: Set to true to disable BGP MPLS forwarding. (leaf)

multihop.description:
Describes the number of IP hops allowed
between a given BGP neighbor and
the PE. (leaf)

redistribute-connected.description:
Indicates, per address family, the
policy to follow for connected
routes. (list)

enable.description:
Enables the redistribution of
connected routes. (leaf)

peer-as.description:
Indicates the customer's ASN when
the customer requests BGP routing. (leaf)

items.description:
IP address(es) of the BGP neighbor.
IPv4 and IPv6 neighbors may be
indicated if two sessions will be used
for IPv4 and IPv6. (leaf-list)

address-family.description:
This node contains the address families
to be activated.  'dual-stack' means
that both IPv4 and IPv6 will be
activated. (leaf)

id.description:
Unique identifier for the routing
protocol. (leaf)

qos-profile.description:
QoS profile.
Can be a standard profile or
a customized profile. (list)

direction.description:
The direction to which the QoS
profile is applied. (leaf)

mtu.description:
MTU at the service level.  If the service
is IP, it refers to the IP MTU.  If
Carriers' Carriers (CsC) is enabled, the
requested MTU will refer to the MPLS
maximum labeled packet size and not to the
IP MTU. (leaf)

prefix-length.description:
Subnet prefix length expressed in bits.
It is applied to both local and customer
addresses. (leaf)

local-address.description:
The IP address used at the provider's
interface. (leaf)

prefix-length.description:
Subnet prefix length expressed in bits.
It is applied to both local and customer
addresses. (leaf)

local-address.description: IPv6 address of the provider side. (leaf)

type.description:
Encapsulation type.  By default, the type
of the tagged interface is
'priority-tagged'. (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:BDI.description: <1-4095> BDI interface number. BDI must be unique for each sub-interface on the device. (leaf)

connection.description:
Defines Layer 2 protocols and parameters that
are required to enable connectivity between
the PE and the CE. (non-presence)

oam.description:
Defines the Operations, Administration,
and Maintenance (OAM) mechanisms used.

BFD is set as a fault detection mechanism,
but other mechanisms can be defined in the
future. (non-presence)

interface-id.description:
Identifier for the physical or logical
interface.
The identification of the sub-interface
is provided at the connection level and/or
the IP connection level. (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:is-receiver-site-only.description: Current PE has only receiver sites. (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:policy-name.description: Name of the SR P2MP Policy (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:multicast.description: Multicast parameters for the vpn node. (non-presence)

local-as.description:
Provider's AS number.  Used if the customer
requests BGP routing. (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:custom-template.description: Custom templates to apply when device is ready (list)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:variable.description: Variables to bind when applying template one time (list)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:iteration.description: Apply custom template multiple times with iteration (list)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:variable.description: Variables to bind when applying template in iteration (list)

cisco-mvpn:mvpn-ipmsi-tunnel-ipv4.description:
Default mdt or I-PMSI configuration and
operational state information (non-presence)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:count.description: No. of group addresses to join (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:inc-mask.description: Increment mask for group or source address (presence)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:count.description: No. of group addresses to join (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:inc-mask.description: Increment mask for group or source address (presence)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:policy-name.description: Name of the SR P2MP Policy (leaf)

cisco-mvpn:mvpn-spmsi-tunnel-ipv4.description:
S-PMSI tunnel attributes configuration and
operational state information. (list)

cisco-mvpn:immediate-switch.description: To enable to switch to Data Mdt immediately (leaf)

cisco-mvpn:group-acl-ipv4.description:
Specify the (s, g) entry on which the
S-PMSI tunnel takes effect.
The value is an integer ranging from 3000
to 3999 or a string of 32 case-sensitive
characters. If no value is specified, the
switch-group address pool takes effect on
all (s, g). (leaf)

cisco-mvpn:per-item-tunnel-limit.description:
Maximum number of S-PMSI tunnels allowed
per S-PMSI configuration item per mvpn instance. (leaf)

cisco-mvpn:route-policy.description: Route policy to select data mdt core (leaf)

cisco-mvpn:switch-threshold.description:
Multicast packet rate threshold for
triggering the switching from the
I-PMSI to the S-PMSI. The value is
an integer ranging from 0 to 4194304, in
kbps. The default value is 1. (leaf)

cisco-mvpn:switch-wildcard-mode.description:
I-PMSI switching to S-PMSI mode for private net
wildcard mode, which including (*,*), (*,G), (S,*),
(S,G) four modes. (leaf)

cisco-mvpn:mvpn-spmsi-tunnels-ipv4.description:
S-PMSI tunnel configuration and
operational state information. (non-presence)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:profile-name.description:
Please input profile name in following format: '<profile-name> (custom|system)'
 The possible profile names for system are: Gold_L2VPN_ConfigProfile, Gold_L3VPN_ConfigProfile, Silver_L2VPN_ConfigProfile, Silver_L3VPN_ConfigProfile. (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:monitoring-state.description: To enable, disable or pause the service assurance monitoring (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:preservation.description: Preserve/Remove all assurance related historical data after stop monitoring (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:rule-name.description:
Please input rule name in following format: '<rule-name> (custom|system)'
 The possible rule names for system are: Rule-L2VPN-NM, Rule-L2VPN-NM-Basic, Rule-L2VPN-NM-P2P, Rule-L2VPN-NM-P2P-Basic, Rule-L2VPN-MP, Rule-L2VPN-MP-Basic, Rule-L3VPN-NM, Rule-L3VPN-NM-Basic. (leaf)

rd.description: Indicates an RD value that is explicitly assigned. (leaf)

role.description: Role of the VPN node in the VPN. (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:locator-name.description: SRv6 locator name, should match locators configured at a node-global level on each router (leaf)

address-family.description: Set of parameters per address family. (list)

vpn-instance-profiles.description: Container for a list of VPN instance profiles. (non-presence)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:sr-policy-ref.description: List of sr-policies (static or dynamically created) (list)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:discovered-underlay-tran.description: HCO service to underlay transport mapping (non-presence)

instance-type.description:
Indicates a transport instance type.  For example,
it can be a VPN+, an IETF network slice, a virtual
network, etc. (leaf)

transport-instance-id.description:
An optional identifier of the abstract transport
instance. (leaf)

vpn-id.description:
A VPN identifier that uniquely identifies a VPN.
This identifier has a local meaning, e.g., within
a service provider network. (leaf)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:connection.description: List of point to point based connections (list)

cisco-l3vpn-ntw:point-to-point.description: Includes point to point based connections (non-presence)

last-change.description:
Indicates the actual date and time of the service status
change. (leaf)
